```{r}
hpc.simple.lmm <- lmer(Avg_Hippocampal_volume_adjusted ~ Time*AgeBinary*Sex + Natural_environment_1000m_10pcnt + time_between_MRIs + time_baseline_to_first_MRI + BMI + Education + Income + Average_time_outdoors + Summed_PA_MET_min + Smoking + Disability + Deprivation_Index + Population_density + CVD_problems + WMH_firstMRI + (1 | ID), BrainCovWMHTownsend.long3, REML=FALSE)

Anova(hpc.simple.lmm, type="III")

# Estimated brain changes over Time, overall
emm <- emmeans(hpc.simple.lmm, "Time",weights="equal")
confint(pairs(emm))

# Estimated brain changes over Time, by Age*Sex group 
emm <- emmeans(hpc.simple.lmm, ~ Time | Sex | AgeBinary)
time_contrast1 <- contrast(emm,method="revpairwise",by=c("Sex","AgeBinary"))
confint(time_contrast1)

# Estimated brain changes over Time, by Age group only
emm <- emmeans(hpc.simple.lmm, ~ Time | AgeBinary)
time_contrast2 <- contrast(emm,method="revpairwise",by="AgeBinary")
confint(time_contrast2)

# Estimated brain changes over Time, by Sex only
emm <- emmeans(hpc.simple.lmm, ~ Time | Sex)
time_contrast3 <- contrast(emm,method="revpairwise",by="Sex")
confint(time_contrast3)
```

## 9.4) WMH volume

ANOVA on change scores with combined Age*Sex grouping variable (no covariates)
```{r}
wmh.change.aov1 <- lm(Total_WMH_volume_adjusted_CHANGE ~ SexAgeBinary, data=explainAgeSex)

Anova(wmh.change.aov1, type="III") #p=0.91 for Age*Sex 

# Estimated brain changes, overall
emmeans(lm(Total_WMH_volume_adjusted_CHANGE ~ 1, data=explainAgeSex), ~1)

# Estimated brain changes by Age*Sex group 
summary(pairs(emmeans(wmh.change.aov1, ~ SexAgeBinary), adjust = "tukey"), infer=c(TRUE,TRUE))
```

ANOVA on change scores with combined Age*Sex grouping variable (no covariates)
```{r}
wmh.change.aov1 <- lm(Total_WMH_volume_adjusted_CHANGE ~ SexAgeBinary, data=explainAgeSex)

Anova(wmh.change.aov1, type="III") #p=0.91 for Age*Sex 

# Estimated brain changes, overall
emmeans(lm(Total_WMH_volume_adjusted_CHANGE ~ 1, data=explainAgeSex), ~1)

# Estimated brain changes by Age*Sex group 
pairs(emmeans(wmh.change.aov1, ~ SexAgeBinary), adjust = "tukey")
```

RM-ANOVA on scores at each timepoint (equivalent to LMM without covariates and with random intercepts only)
```{r}
wmh.rmaov <- lmer(Total_WMH_volume_adjusted ~ Time*AgeBinary*Sex + (1 | ID), BrainCovWMHTownsend.long3, REML=FALSE)

Anova(wmh.rmaov, type="III")

# Estimated brain changes over Time, overall
emm <- emmeans(wmh.rmaov, "Time",weights="equal")
confint(pairs(emm))

# Estimated brain changes over Time, by Age*Sex group 
emm <- emmeans(wmh.rmaov, ~ Time | Sex | AgeBinary)
time_contrast1 <- contrast(emm,method="revpairwise",by=c("Sex","AgeBinary"))
confint(time_contrast1)

  #Post-hoc comparisons
  ##Differences across Time, between Sexes, within Age groups
  emm <- emmeans(wmh.rmaov, ~ Time * Sex | AgeBinary)
  time_contrast <- contrast(emm,interaction=c("pairwise","pairwise"), by="AgeBinary")
  summary(time_contrast)
  
  ##Differences across Time, between Age groups, within Sexes
  emm <- emmeans(wmh.rmaov, ~ Time * AgeBinary | Sex)
  time_contrast <- contrast(emm,interaction=c("pairwise","pairwise"), by="Sex")
  summary(time_contrast)

# Estimated brain changes over Time, by Age group only
emm <- emmeans(wmh.rmaov, ~ Time | AgeBinary)
time_contrast2 <- contrast(emm,method="revpairwise",by="AgeBinary")
confint(time_contrast2)

# Estimated brain changes over Time, by Sex only
emm <- emmeans(wmh.rmaov, ~ Time | Sex)
time_contrast3 <- contrast(emm,method="revpairwise",by="Sex")
confint(time_contrast3)
```


LMM including covariates
```{r}
wmh.simple.lmm <- lmer(Grey_matter_volume_adjusted ~ Time*AgeBinary*Sex + Natural_environment_1000m_10pcnt + time_between_MRIs + time_baseline_to_first_MRI + BMI + Education + Income + Average_time_outdoors + Summed_PA_MET_min + Smoking + Disability + Deprivation_Index + Population_density + CVD_problems + WMH_firstMRI + (1 | ID), BrainCovWMHTownsend.long3, REML=FALSE)

Anova(wmh.simple.lmm, type="III")

# Estimated brain changes over Time, overall
emm <- emmeans(wmh.simple.lmm, "Time",weights="equal")
confint(pairs(emm))

# Estimated brain changes over Time, by Age*Sex group 
emm <- emmeans(wmh.simple.lmm, ~ Time | Sex | AgeBinary)
time_contrast1 <- contrast(emm,method="revpairwise",by=c("Sex","AgeBinary"))
confint(time_contrast1)

# Estimated brain changes over Time, by Age group only
emm <- emmeans(wmh.simple.lmm, ~ Time | AgeBinary)
time_contrast2 <- contrast(emm,method="revpairwise",by="AgeBinary")
confint(time_contrast2)

# Estimated brain changes over Time, by Sex only
emm <- emmeans(wmh.simple.lmm, ~ Time | Sex)
time_contrast3 <- contrast(emm,method="revpairwise",by="Sex")
confint(time_contrast3)
```

# 10) Extra tests comparing baseline variables between Age/Sex groups

## Tests for categorical descriptive variables condensed to binary: Ethnicity, Education, Houshold Income, Population density, Smoking Status

```{r}
#Create new binary variables; use explainAgeSex dataset

#Ethnicity
explainAgeSex$EthnicityBinary[explainAgeSex$Race=="White"] <- "White"
explainAgeSex$EthnicityBinary[explainAgeSex$Race=="Mixed" | explainAgeSex$Race=="Asian or Asian British" | explainAgeSex$Race=="Black or Black British"] <- "Not White"

#Education
explainAgeSex$EducationBinary[explainAgeSex$Education=="College or University Degree"] <- "College or University Degree"
explainAgeSex$EducationBinary[explainAgeSex$Education=="None of the above" | explainAgeSex$Education=="CSEs or equivalent" | explainAgeSex$Education=="O levels/General Certificate of Secondary Education or equivalent" | explainAgeSex$Education=="A levels/AS levels or equivalent" | explainAgeSex$Education=="NVQ or HND or HNC or equivalent" | explainAgeSex$Education=="Other professional qualifications"] <- "Other" 

#Home area population density
explainAgeSex$UrbanRural[explainAgeSex$Population_density=="Urban area"] <- "Urban"
explainAgeSex$UrbanRural[explainAgeSex$Population_density=="Town" | explainAgeSex$Population_density=="Village, Hamlet, or Rural Area"] <- "Town, Village, Hamlet, or Rural"

#Smoking status
explainAgeSex$SmokingBinary[explainAgeSex$Smoking=="Current Smoker" | explainAgeSex$Smoking=="Previous Smoker"] <- "Smoked"
explainAgeSex$SmokingBinary[explainAgeSex$Smoking=="Never"] <- "Never"

#Household income
explainAgeSex$IncomeBinary[explainAgeSex$Income=="52,000-100,000 GBP" | explainAgeSex$Income==">100,000 GBP"] <- ">=52,000 GBP"
explainAgeSex$IncomeBinary[explainAgeSex$Income=="<18,000 GBP" | explainAgeSex$Income=="18,000-30,999 GBP" | explainAgeSex$Income=="31,000-51,999 GBP"] <- "<52,000 GBP"


# Table to compare descriptives between high and low age
tbl_summary(
  dplyr::select(explainAgeSex, c(52:57)),
  by = "SexAgeBinary", 
  statistic = list(all_continuous() ~ "{mean} ({sd})",
                   all_categorical() ~ "{n} ({p}%)"),
  digits = all_continuous() ~ 3
) %>%
  add_p(test = list(all_continuous() ~ "aov",
                   all_categorical() ~ "chisq.test"),
        pvalue_fun = ~ style_pvalue(.x, digits = 3))
```

## Extra post-hoc tests for baseline variables by Age/Sex groups

Household income: Chi-squared tests for Age*Sex grouping variable: overall and pairwise
```{r}
#overall across all 4 groups
test <- chisq.test(explainAgeSex$SexAgeBinary, explainAgeSex$Income)
test$stdres

#younger M versus younger F
explainAgeSex.young <- subset(explainAgeSex, SexAgeBinary==c("Males aYounger", "Females aYounger"))
chisq.test(explainAgeSex.young$Sex, explainAgeSex.young$Income) #not significant p=0.714

#older M versus older F
explainAgeSex.old <- subset(explainAgeSex, SexAgeBinary==c("Males zOlder", "Females zOlder"))
chisq.test(explainAgeSex.old$Sex, explainAgeSex.old$Income) #significant; p=0.0034
```


Hearing difficulties: ANOVA on baseline scores with combined Age*Sex grouping variable (no covariates)
```{r}
#overall across all 4 groups
chisq.test(explainAgeSex$SexAgeBinary, explainAgeSex$Hearing_difficulty)
test$stdres

#younger M versus younger F
chisq.test(explainAgeSex.young$Sex, explainAgeSex.young$Hearing_difficulty) #not significant; p=0.812

#older M versus older F
chisq.test(explainAgeSex.old$Sex, explainAgeSex.old$Hearing_difficulty) #not significant; p=0.073
```

Grey matter volume: ANOVA on baseline scores with combined Age*Sex grouping variable (no covariates)
```{r}
gm.baseline.aov1 <- lm(Grey_matter_volume_adjusted_T2 ~ SexAgeBinary, data=explainAgeSex)

Anova(gm.baseline.aov1, type="III") 

# Estimated differences by Age*Sex group 
summary(pairs(emmeans(gm.baseline.aov1, ~ SexAgeBinary), adjust = "tukey"),infer=c(TRUE,TRUE))
```

White matter volume: ANOVA on baseline scores with combined Age*Sex grouping variable (no covariates)
```{r}
wm.baseline.aov1 <- lm(White_matter_volume_adjusted_T2 ~ SexAgeBinary, data=explainAgeSex)

Anova(wm.baseline.aov1, type="III") 

# Estimated differences by Age*Sex group 
summary(pairs(emmeans(wm.baseline.aov1, ~ SexAgeBinary), adjust = "tukey"),infer=c(TRUE,TRUE))
```

HPC volume: ANOVA on baseline scores with combined Age*Sex grouping variable (no covariates)
```{r}
hpc.baseline.aov1 <- lm(Avg_Hippocampal_volume_adjusted_T2 ~ SexAgeBinary, data=explainAgeSex)

Anova(hpc.baseline.aov1, type="III") 

# Estimated differences by Age*Sex group 
summary(pairs(emmeans(hpc.baseline.aov1, ~ SexAgeBinary), adjust = "tukey"),infer=c(TRUE,TRUE))
```

WMH volume: ANOVA on baseline scores with combined Age*Sex grouping variable (no covariates)
```{r}
wmh.baseline.aov1 <- lm(Total_WMH_volume_adjusted_T2 ~ SexAgeBinary, data=explainAgeSex)

Anova(wmh.baseline.aov1, type="III") 

# Estimated differences by Age*Sex group 
summary(pairs(emmeans(wmh.baseline.aov1, ~ SexAgeBinary), adjust = "tukey"),infer=c(TRUE,TRUE))
```
